recommendation.reasoning: The recommendation to not pursue the SearchBoost Query Engine is driven by significant risks related to authentication weaknesses and potential handling of personally identifiable information (PII), which are critical blockers [risk-001, risk-002]. Additionally, the project faces unmitigated high risks that exceed acceptable limits, alongside critical issues such as a drop in search relevance and increased support ticket volume, indicating that the product could negatively impact user experience and operational efficiency [customer-001, feasibility-001, customer-002].

executive_summary:
The SearchBoost Query Engine is a proposed product designed to enhance search relevance, particularly for anonymous users, addressing a critical opportunity identified in our customer feedback [customer-001]. This product aims to improve user experience by reducing irrelevant search results and enhancing overall search performance. However, our analysis of 34 findings has revealed significant concerns, particularly regarding authentication weaknesses and potential handling of personally identifiable information (PII) [risk-001], [risk-002]. These issues have raised red flags that necessitate a thorough review before any further investment.

The analysis has highlighted several top risks, including three unmitigated high risks that exceed our acceptable threshold, leading to a failed risk gate [risk-001], [risk-002]. Key opportunities for improvement were identified, such as addressing the significant drop in search relevance for anonymous users and the increased support ticket volume due to irrelevant results [customer-001], [customer-002]. However, the trade-offs associated with these improvements, particularly the legal implications surrounding PII handling, present substantial barriers to moving forward with this product at this time.

Given the high level of risk and the potential legal ramifications, the recommendation is to not pursue the SearchBoost Query Engine at this stage, with a confidence level of 85% in this decision. Immediate next steps for the team include conducting a comprehensive legal review to address the flagged PII handling issues and reassessing the product's feasibility in light of the identified risks. Additionally, we should explore alternative solutions that can enhance search relevance without compromising user data security.

key_findings.title: Significant Drop in Search Relevance for Anonymous Users
key_findings.description: The recent deployment of v2.4.1 has led to a 15% drop in search relevance scores, disproportionately affecting anonymous users who make up approximately 40% of query volume. This segment is experiencing a significant degradation in search result relevance due to the new ranking algorithm, which relies on historical session data unavailable for non-authenticated requests.
recommendations: Re-run A/B test scoped to anonymous users with a properly powered sample size.
Patch ranking model for anonymous user fallback path.
assumptions: Anonymous users are a significant portion of the user base.

key_findings.title: Search Relevance Score Drop
key_findings.description: The recent deployment (v2.4.1) has resulted in a significant drop in search relevance scores, which could lead to user dissatisfaction and increased support volume. Immediate action is required to analyze and remediate the issue.
recommendations: Conduct a root-cause analysis of the ranking algorithm.
Consider a rollback if remediation is not feasible.
assumptions: The drop in relevance is directly related to the new ranking algorithm introduced in v2.4.1.

key_findings.title: North Star Metric: Search Relevance Score
key_findings.description: The North Star metric for SearchBoost is the Search Relevance Score, which measures the effectiveness of search results as perceived by users. This metric is critical for understanding user satisfaction and engagement with search results.
recommendations: Monitor search relevance scores closely and investigate factors contributing to the drop.
assumptions: Search relevance directly correlates with user satisfaction and engagement.

key_findings.title: Instrumentation Gap for New Query Types
key_findings.description: There is a significant gap in data collection for new query types introduced in v2.4.1, specifically image search and voice search. Without instrumentation for these query types, we lack visibility into their performance and user engagement.
recommendations: Implement baseline instrumentation for image search and voice search to enable future analysis.
assumptions: Instrumentation is necessary for measuring the success of new features.

key_findings.title: Improve Search Relevance for Anonymous Users
key_findings.description: Address the 15% drop in search relevance scores for anonymous users by adjusting the ranking algorithm to better accommodate non-authenticated queries.
recommendations: Re-evaluate the ranking algorithm for anonymous users.
assumptions: The current ranking algorithm is not suitable for anonymous users.

key_findings.title: Increased Support Ticket Volume Due to Irrelevant Results
key_findings.description: Support ticket volume has increased threefold since the v2.4.1 deployment, primarily due to users reporting irrelevant search results. This indicates a critical pain point where users are unable to find relevant information efficiently, leading to frustration and potential churn.
recommendations: Implement immediate fixes to improve search relevance.
Consider a rollback to the previous version if issues persist.
assumptions: Users expect relevant search results based on their queries.

key_findings.title: Mobile Search Latency Increased Significantly
key_findings.description: Search latency on mobile web has increased to 890ms, significantly affecting user experience. This increase in latency is likely contributing to the overall dissatisfaction with the search functionality, particularly for mobile users.
recommendations: Profile ranking re-ranker for mobile session path to identify optimization opportunities.
Implement mobile-specific caching to reduce latency.
assumptions: Users expect fast response times for search queries.

key_findings.title: Feature Flag Dependency for Rollback
key_findings.description: The ability to rollback the v2.4.1 deployment is blocked by a dependent feature flag change. This dependency must be addressed to enable a rollback if necessary.
recommendations: Identify and resolve the feature flag dependency to allow for rollback.
assumptions: The feature flag is critical for the rollback process.

key_findings.title: Increased Support Volume
key_findings.description: The spike in support volume due to irrelevant search results indicates a potential loss of user trust and engagement. This issue needs to be prioritized for resolution.
recommendations: Prioritize the analysis and remediation of the ranking algorithm.
assumptions: User dissatisfaction is directly correlated with the relevance issues.

key_findings.title: Search Latency Optimization
key_findings.description: The increased search latency on mobile web indicates a potential performance issue with the new ranking model. Profiling and optimization are required to ensure performance meets user expectations.
recommendations: Profile the new ranking model and optimize for mobile performance.
assumptions: The new ranking model is causing increased latency.

north_star.title: North Star Metric: Search Relevance Score
north_star.description: The North Star metric for SearchBoost is the Search Relevance Score, which measures the effectiveness of search results as perceived by users. This metric is critical for understanding user satisfaction and engagement with search results.

input_metrics.title: Primary KPI: Search Click-Through Rate (CTR)
input_metrics.description: The Search Click-Through Rate (CTR) measures the percentage of search results that users click on after performing a search. This is a key indicator of the effectiveness of search results in attracting user engagement.

guardrails.title: Guardrail Metric: Session Abandonment After Search
guardrails.description: This metric tracks the percentage of sessions that are abandoned after a search is performed. A high abandonment rate may indicate dissatisfaction with search results.
